_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Analyze user needs and software requirements to determine feasibility of design within time and cost constraints.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design technical implementations that balance immediate delivery requirements with long-term maintainability, scalability, and operational sustainability.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-A-001 (DA, cosine: 0.460), DQ-A-004 (DQ, cosine: 0.457).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.445). Shared vocabulary: [design, requirements]. DCWF-only terms [analyze, constraints, cost, determine, feasibility, needs] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, balance, delivery, immediate, implementations, long] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.875 (P=0.884, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:35.7%, N:64.1%, C:0.2%). No dominant classification for "Analyze user needs and software requirements to determine feasibility of design" vs "Ability to design technical implementations that balance immediate delivery requ" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.511 > STS 0.445 (gap +0.066). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [design, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.8%. Shared tokens: [design, requirements]. DCWF-only: [analyze, constraints, cost, determine, feasibility, needs]. WIDAI-only: [ability, balance, delivery, immediate, implementations, long].
